title: ScoreReport
description: Automated performance scoring for one completed encounter, produced by the scoring engine and shown at the FEEDBACK stage. Deterministic, rule-based scoring is authoritative; the LLM only authors narrative feedback text, never the numbers.

esi.description: The headline result. triageDirection is the project's core metric: under-triage (assigning a less acute level than expert) is the dangerous error the tool exists to reduce.

triageDirection.description: UNDER_TRIAGE = assigned a higher (less acute) number than expert => patient under-prioritized. The headline safety signal.

levelsOff.description: assigned - expert. Negative = over-triage, positive = under-triage.

dimensions.description: Per-competency breakdown. Each dimension scored 0..1 with its own weight; overallPercent is the weighted sum.

key.description: ESI_ACCURACY is weighted highest and penalizes UNDER_TRIAGE more than OVER_TRIAGE. OUTCOME_ALIGNMENT is skipped (weight 0) when the case has no real outcome.

detail.description: Human-readable explanation of how this dimension scored, e.g. which red flags were missed.

narrative.description: LLM-authored teaching feedback grounded in the dimensions above. Must reference the numeric findings; never invents scores.

missedRedFlags.description: Red flags from the case the trainee never elicited or acted on.
